_meta.description: Patient-completed outcome measures (PROMs) for the in-clinic kiosk (Phase 4). Each PROM defines its items (or clinician-entered activities), a 0-N tappable scale, a deterministic `scoring` shape, and interpretation `bands`. Scoring is computed server-side (services/prom-scoring.js), never by the LLM. Seed ONLY license-safe instruments — many PROMs are copyrighted for electronic/commercial use. `license` records the basis per instrument; confirm before commercial go-live.

scoring.average: Score = mean of the (clinician-entered) activity ratings (PSFS).
scoring.sum: Score = sum of item values (e.g. LEFS).

proms.name: Numeric Pain Rating Scale
proms.patientIntro: Please rate your pain over the past week.

items.text: On average over the past week, how bad has your pain been?

proms.name: Patient-Specific Functional Scale
proms.license: Free for clinical use (Stratford et al. 1995).
proms.patientIntro: For each activity, rate how able you are to do it today.

activities.prompt: Activity the patient finds difficult (e.g. walking 20 min, climbing stairs)
